name: Improper Clearing of Heap Memory Before Release ('Heap Inspection')
description: Sensitive data such as passwords or encryption keys remain in heap memory after reallocation because realloc() copies contents to a new block but leaves the original data intact. Attackers can exploit this through heap inspection attacks using memory dumps to recover secrets.
detection_pattern: Look for STORAGE nodes (heap buffers holding sensitive data like passwords, keys) that flow through TRANSFORM nodes (realloc, free) to EGRESS nodes (released memory pages) without a CONTROL mediator (explicit memory scrubbing, memset/SecureZeroMemory before free) that clears sensitive data before releasing the memory.
